description: A plain, undesigned candid nighttime photograph (second image in the same 'REAL TRUTH vs FAKE NEWS' Minneapolis/Hennepin County tweet). It shows the front end of a dark blue/black Honda sedan with significant front-end collision damage: the front bumper cover is partially torn off and hanging, the grille and lower fascia are cracked and crumpled, and the headlight area is damaged, consistent with a crash. The vehicle sits on a slushy, snow-and-sand-covered roadway at night; faint shadows (likely of the person photographing) fall across the foreground, and reddish vehicle lights/another vehicle are dimly visible in the background. There is no on-image text, no seal, no graphic design, typography, or overlay; it is a raw evidentiary smartphone-style snapshot documenting damage to the vehicle to support the agency's account of the incident. Not a meme or designed graphic. (Note: the OCR text for this asset is spurious numeric/log data and does not reflect visible on-image text.)
